---
title: "Bioinformatics"
page-layout: full
toc: true
---

#

![](images/lab1.jpg){width=100%}

---

# Overview

Throughout my research, bioinformatics has been an essential tool for analysing environmental sequencing data and investigating microbial communities. I have worked extensively with the DADA2 metabarcoding pipeline on high-performance computing (HPC) systems to process Illumina sequencing datasets and have gained experience analysing Oxford Nanopore sequencing data using a range of bioinformatics workflows. 

---

## What I am curious about

- Can artificial intelligence help uncover ecological patterns and microbial interactions that remain hidden within complex environmental sequencing datasets?

- Can advances in AI and bioinformatics accelerate the discovery of microbial functions that support resilient ecosystems?

---

# Research Areas

My bioinformatics work spans multiple sequencing platforms and ecosystems, including Arctic marine microbial ecology, soil microbiomes (soon to be), cryopegs, pingos, permafrost and so on...

Projects have included:

- Environmental DNA metabarcoding
- Oxford Nanopore sequencing
- Illumina amplicon sequencing
- Long-term microbial time series
- Community ecology analyses

---

# Tools & Software

### Programming

- R (advanced)
- Python (basics)
- Bash (advanced)


### Computing

- High Performance Computing (HPC)
- Linux
- Slurm

---

# Skills

- Reproducible workflows
- Community ecology
- High-performance computing
- Data visualization
- Environmental DNA
